title: The type EP2 salvage of Shinji in episode 20
display_title: The type EP2 salvage of Shinji in episode 20
wikitext:
[]: The "type EP2 salvage" of Shinji in episode #20             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Jan 06, 2008 11:57 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 06, 2008 12:04 pm Wow, excellent job so far Zugz! I like it! :)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 06, 2008 12:15 pm This analysis should be put in the T&A section of the Wiki (assuming Reichu and co. approves). It's a good analysis, but to be honest, there really isn't anything in it I didn't already understand. Shinji absorbed completely into Sho's core, essentially returning to the womb, they try to pull him out (and fail), but Shinji is re-born anyway, either through his own will or Yui's. BTW, the red/blue transition/significance is something I've been preaching for a while. Nobody ever seems to like my ideas about color symbolism, so I stopped pressing it (I really need to go through the series and collect all the data... there's a lot).

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 06, 2008 7:14 pm Wow, somebody has had a lot of time on their hands. Good work. Finally something new in the discussion room. Now, I may be wrong, but would the Klein Space be related in anyway to how Half-Life's Portal game works? Like where you exit on the right side, and enter on the left?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jan 07, 2008 12:16 am Sachiel_13 wrote: Wow, somebody has had a lot of time on their hands. Good work. Finally something new in the discussion room. Now, I may be wrong, but would the Klein Space be related in anyway to how Half-Life's Portal game works? Like where you exit on the right side, and enter on the left?

[]:             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Mon Jan 07, 2008 9:19 am Hmmmm... After reading UA's 'The Rebirth of Yui Ikari' fanfic, I have to wonder...if Shinji's soul HAD been fully absorbed into Eva 01, would it have been Yui who'd be born from the core? I imagine only one soul can inhabit an Eva at a time. Guess it's sort of a digression. I just thought a Shingouki would be AWESOME. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Mon Jan 07, 2008 3:34 pm Oh my, this is great, thanks so much for posting. I can't believe I didn't notice the whole entry plug color thing before, but I totally understand. Eva Yojimbo, I'm surprised you got shut down on the color symbolism front, I think it makes a great deal of sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jan 07, 2008 4:13 pm --

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jan 07, 2008 4:50 pm Zugzwang wrote: There's also that bit of animation of the seashore and waves seen from underwater that comes up again and again in the show that I don't remember anyone making a stab of explaining yet...

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jan 07, 2008 5:16 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Take how blue is associated with water. Water is associated with LCL. Being in LCL in the Evas is like being back in the womb. Hence, returning to the comfort of the mother (of course, this is all encompassed through Rei, who is an amalgamation of both Yui and Lilith, the two "mothers"). Red crops up every time things are going to shit in reality. Ep. 3, 4, 7, and EoE are just the one's I've noticed so far. But you obviously have to note the constant contrasting of red and blue throughout the series. And the choice Shinji has to make in the end is between staying in that "Sea of LCL with/through Rei", or returning to the "purified Red Earth with Asuka". Coinkydink? I think not.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Jan 07, 2008 5:48 pm Zugzwang wrote: Asuka: " RED or BLUE , Shinji, make up your mind!"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 07, 2008 7:15 pm @Yojimbo: The red/blue association falls apart a little bit if you consider how the Sea of LCL scene starts off red, then shifts to blue/green once Shinji starts contemplating about how Instrumentality sucks. We then revisit the blue sky imagery from EoTV. In EoTV, there isn't exactly a red/blue dichotomy, but the world yielded when the closed world of Instrumentality shatters is, well, made of blues and greens. In the opening sequence, which seems to represent a view of the creation of the universe, all begins with a blue "cosmic spark" that yields to "red space", which in turn transitions into "blue space" and eventually furnishes water imagery. The "flickering light on water" image is apparently supposed to represent what the world looks like from within the womb.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jan 07, 2008 7:35 pm Reichu wrote: The red/blue association falls apart a little bit if you consider how the Sea of LCL scene starts off red, then shifts to blue/green once Shinji starts contemplating about how Instrumentality sucks. We then revisit the blue sky imagery from EoTV.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jan 09, 2008 10:40 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Jan 09, 2008 6:47 pm Zugzwang wrote: what do you think of the waves landing on the shore with the technicolour sands?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Jan 10, 2008 11:03 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Jan 10, 2008 7:21 pm Zugzwang wrote: When folks questioned your essay length responses on A.N. you said you merely typed them up out of boredom while you were working, so I don't see why doing that would be such a problem for you now.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 9:28 pm Wait a moment there...if the Misato, Asuka and Rei seen by Shinji when he was tanged were all fronts for Yui and not part of Ritsuko's program...what gives? Yui wanted to merge with Shinji, did she? Leave out the other two Angels, would she have started off 3I if Shinji decided to merge with her after all? Also, she breaks out of the bakelite and lets Shinji inside in EoE: Essentially, she wants him aboard for instrumentality. Since Yui wanted a child way back when she was a student, maybe...maybe she just wanted to be one with Shinji again?(Gendo says "It's started" or something like that when Eva-01 snaps the remaining bindings and bellows) I admit that the idea of Yui wanting to start off the process as soon as getting her son and an S2 would make a lot of sense, and so would the idea of Yui being a rather lonely young woman who saw the best in others because she wanted their company. It'd be quite in line with what the other characters in NGE are like. But why does Shinji emerge from the Eva? He says "mother" and then he's out...has anyone come to a consensus over what was happening in there during Episode 20?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jan 23, 2008 8:50 am --

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Jan 23, 2008 9:08 pm The core doesn't look any different seconds after Shinji was flushed out of it. Anyways, do you think Yui was trying to merge with Shinji so she could comfort him and help him with his troubles? Like, a last ditch effort to try and help her son...like a trippy type of rehab?
